meta.title: Isla Café — Your island corner in Miami
meta.description: Specialty coffee, Caribbean brunch, and neighborhood warmth in Cutler Bay, Miami.

hero.subtitle: Specialty coffee, Caribbean brunch, and neighborhood warmth — made fresh every morning.

historia.label: Our story
historia.titleLine1: A corner where Miami feels like
historia.body: Isla was born from a family kitchen and the idea that good coffee is meant to be shared. We roast in small batches, bake every morning, and serve the cortadito the right way — unhurried, with care.

pillars.title: Roasted in-house
pillars.body: Single-origin beans from small farms, roasted here in small batches every week.

pillars.title: Baked daily
pillars.body: Guava pastelitos, croissants, and tres leches, handmade before we open.

pillars.title: Neighborhood table
pillars.body: A place to stay awhile: neighbors, laptops, dominoes, and long conversations.

items.name: Café con Leche
items.description: Double espresso, steamed milk, caramelized sugar.

items.name: Cortadito
items.description: Miami's signature shot — espresso cut with evaporated milk.

items.name: House Cold Brew
items.description: Steeped 18 hours, notes of cacao and cane.

items.name: Tostada Isla
items.description: Sourdough, avocado, poached egg, ají amarillo.

items.name: Guava Pastelito
items.description: Flaky puff pastry, guava and cream cheese.

espacio.label: The space
espacio.title: Morning light, plants, and the aroma of fresh roast.

featured.quote: The best cortadito south of Miami. I come every morning before work.
featured.author: Marisol R.

featured.quote: The tostada isla and a cold brew — my Sunday ritual. It feels like home.
featured.author: Daniel & Cris

featured.quote: Warm pastelitos, warm people, and real wifi. My favorite Miami office.
featured.author: Priya N.

reviews.name: Ana Lucía
reviews.text: Spectacular coffee and the staff treats you like family. Pastelitos sell out — come early.

reviews.name: Jorge M.
reviews.text: The house cold brew is on another level. Calm vibe, perfect for getting work done.

reviews.name: Sophia T.
reviews.text: My new go-to spot. The mallorca french toast is sinful. I'll be back every weekend.

reviews.name: Rob D.
reviews.text: Authentic, welcoming, and unpretentious. Cortadito like my grandmother made. 10/10.

visitanos.mapTitle: Map of Isla Café — 18901 SW 106th Ave, Cutler Bay

newsletter.title: Get the weekly special.
newsletter.body: New roasts, seasonal brunch, and live music nights — no spam, just the good stuff.

items.question: Do you take groups or reservations?
items.answer: Absolutely. For groups of 6+ we recommend reserving. DM us or call and we'll save you the big table by the window.

items.question: Do you have vegan and gluten-free options?
items.answer: Yes — oat and coconut milk at no extra charge, gluten-free toast, and several plant-based brunch options. Ask the team about today's special.

items.question: Do you sell whole bean coffee?
items.answer: We roast weekly and sell 340g bags at the bar and online. Ask about the monthly subscription — freshly roasted, delivered to your door.

items.question: Is there wifi and outlets for working?
items.answer: Fast free wifi and outlets at most tables. We only ask that you free up the large tables during peak brunch hours (Sat–Sun 10 AM–1 PM).

footer.tagline: Your island corner in Cutler Bay. Coffee roasted in-house, Caribbean brunch, and plenty of calm.
